meaning: adj.不安全的,危险的,不安稳的,
examples: In the larger neighbourhood, I felt very unsafe.
It was considered unsafe to release the prisoners.
Discharge of raw sewage into the sea is unsanitary and unsafe.
Of course, if fares are low enough, people will put up with just about anything except unsafe planes.
Away from the factory floor, worries about unsafe poultry are already high in China.
